articles.title: School vacation extension under consideration amid heatwave ; Education Minister - The Kashmir Monitor
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Manipur
articles.full_text:
Srinagar: Jammu and Kashmir Education Minister Sakina Itoo on Saturday said the government will take a decision soon on extending the summer vacation for schools in the valley in view of the ongoing heatwave conditions.
“The government is monitoring the situation closely. Schools are closed till Sunday, and we will take an appropriate decision regarding the vacation, keeping in mind the safety and well-being of our children,” Itoo told reporters .
The education minister said the valley has been recording unusually high temperatures over the past two days, which were not witnessed in previous years.
“Education is important, but the lives and safety of students are our top priority,” the minister added.
Minister Itoo said the decision would be taken after assessing the prevailing weather conditions to ensure that students were not exposed to extreme heat.
Notably, schools in the Kashmir division are currently observing summer vacations, which were earlier announced from July 6 to July 19, 2026.
